senses.definition: a surgery that attaches the sigmoid colon to the abdominal wall to help treat rectal prolapse
examples: The surgeon recommended sigmoidopexy to reduce the prolapse.
After sigmoidopexy, the rectum usually stays in place better.

senses.definition: a surgical connection between the sigmoid colon and the rectum
examples: The procedure was described as a sigmoidoproctostomy.
A sigmoidoproctostomy can reroute the bowel after certain illnesses.

senses.definition: a surgery that connects or joins the sigmoid colon and the rectum
examples: After the sigmoidorectostomy, doctors monitored healing closely.
The imaging results supported a sigmoidorectostomy.

senses.definition: a medical instrument used to look inside the rectum and sigmoid colon
examples: A doctor uses a sigmoidoscope to check for polyps.
They inserted the sigmoidoscope gently during the exam.

senses.definition: plural of sigmoidoscope; medical instruments used to look inside the rectum and sigmoid colon
examples: The clinic sterilizes sigmoidoscopes between patients.
Two sigmoidoscopes were prepared for the procedures.

senses.definition: relating to sigmoidoscopy or to viewing the rectum and sigmoid colon with a sigmoidoscope
examples: The sigmoidoscopic exam helped find the cause of bleeding.
Doctors used sigmoidoscopic findings to guide treatment.

senses.definition: plural of sigmoidoscopy; medical examinations using a sigmoidoscope
examples: Many patients have regular sigmoidoscopies.
The hospital schedules sigmoidoscopies for screening and diagnosis.

senses.definition: a medical exam in which a doctor uses a sigmoidoscope to look at the rectum and sigmoid colon
examples: She had a sigmoidoscopy because of stomach pain.
The doctor explained that sigmoidoscopy can detect polyps.

senses.definition: a surgery that creates an opening (stoma) from the sigmoid colon
examples: A sigmoidostomy may be needed after certain bowel problems.
After the sigmoidostomy, the stoma care team helped her.

senses.definition: plural of sigmoid; the sigmoid colon (the part of the large intestine shaped like an S)
examples: The report mentioned a problem in the sigmoids.
Doctors examined the sigmoids during the procedure.

senses.definition: a male given name
examples: Sigmund was the name on the appointment letter.
My grandfather’s name was Sigmund.

senses.definition: something that shows that something else is happening or is true
examples: A fever is a sign of illness.
Dark clouds are a sign that it may rain.

senses.definition: a piece of writing or a picture placed where people can see it to give information
examples: The sign says “No parking.”
I saw a sign for the train station.

senses.definition: to write your name (or make your name) on a document to show agreement or completion
examples: Please sign the form here.
He signed the contract yesterday.

senses.definition: to give written or official approval by signing
examples: The manager will sign the order today.
The doctor signed the prescription.

senses.definition: a symbol used in math, science, or writing (for example + or =)
examples: What does this sign mean?
Use the minus sign in your answer.

senses.definition: a brand name used for products (exact meaning depends on context)
examples: I bought Signa online.
The label says Signa, but I’m not sure which product it is.

senses.definition: able to be signed (for example, a document that you can sign)
examples: The document is signable now.
Make sure the form is signable before submission.

senses.definition: a small sign or marker (rare/technical usage; meaning depends on the source)
examples: The old signacle was still on the wall.
Local maps include a signacle near the entrance.

senses.definition: signs and information placed in public places to help people find things or understand rules
examples: The station has clear signage.
We improved the signage in the parking area.

senses.definition: plural of signage; sets of signs and information in a place
examples: The signages around the building guide visitors.
Some signages were missing after the renovation.

senses.definition: a sign, sound, or message that tells you something
examples: The whistle was a signal to start.
A red light is a signal to stop.

senses.definition: electronic information sent through a system (such as a radio or phone)
examples: The phone shows the signal strength.
The device sends a signal to the receiver.

senses.definition: to show or make clear that something is happening or true
examples: This result signals a change in policy.
Smoke signals danger.

senses.definition: past simple of signal; showed or made clear something
examples: The whistle signaled the start of the race.
Her silence signaled that she disagreed.

senses.definition: a person who receives a signal (in technical or formal contexts)
examples: The signalee responded to the signal.
A signalee must be within range for the message to be received.

senses.definition: a person or device that sends a signal
examples: The signaller used a light to signal across the field.
The signaller sent the message to the receiver.

senses.definition: plural of signaler; people or devices that send signals
examples: Signalers coordinated the response in real time.
Several signalers were active during the test.

senses.definition: a type of language or communication used to send signals (often used in specialized or fictional contexts)
examples: In the story, signalese replaced spoken words.
The group developed a simple signalese for emergencies.

senses.definition: relating to signaletic methods, especially in communication systems that use signals
examples: The signaletic system improved clarity.
They used signaletic signs during the event.

senses.definition: the study or use of systems for sending messages through signals (especially in specialized contexts)
examples: Signaletics focuses on how signals guide human behavior.
The course covered signaletics in communication technology.
